decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Previous attempts to increase spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500.0). Following the strategy shift suggestion, I will now modify the geometry by increasing the heat spreader width to improve lateral heat distribution towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader width expands the cross-sectional area for lateral heat conduction, reducing thermal resistance to the side boundaries without exceeding material property limits.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip peak temperature due to improved lateral spreading.
Temperature gradient across the spreader should decrease.
risk_notes: Ensure the widened spreader (x0=0.25, new width=0.9 -> x1=1.15) remains within the design domain width (1.2).
Diminishing returns may occur if the spreader approaches the domain edges too closely.
